items: / — global navigation, cards, calls to action

gamepad.reason: The application does not expose gamepad input.

interactions.label: Pointer enters a main navigation item

source_evidence.observation: Delegated hover handling recognizes main navigation items and emits the semantic main-nav-hover event.

steps: Click once to unlock browser audio.
Move the pointer across two main navigation links.
browser_evidence.observation: Each newly entered navigation item receives visible hover styling and one quiet nav hover cue; nested child movement does not retrigger it.

visual_alternative.description: The hovered navigation label changes foreground and focus-ring styling.

repetition.details: At most one cue per newly entered item within 90 ms; cue volume is lower than click feedback.

interactions.label: Pointer enters a non-navigation interactive item

source_evidence.observation: The hover classifier excludes main navigation and real tooltip reveals from the general interactive-item event.

steps: Click once to unlock browser audio.
Move between interactive cards and secondary controls.
browser_evidence.observation: Each item has visible hover feedback and the item-hover cue, distinct from main navigation and tooltip cues.

visual_alternative.description: Interactive items visibly change border, background, or elevation on hover.

repetition.details: At most one cue per newly entered item within 90 ms; child pointer transitions are deduplicated.

source_evidence.observation: The sound fires from tooltip open-state transition, not from generic pointerover.

steps: Hover or focus the information affordance until its tooltip opens.
browser_evidence.observation: The visible tooltip and tooltip-show cue occur together exactly once.

visual_alternative.description: Tooltip text is visibly rendered and remains available without audio.

repetition.details: Pointer movement inside an already open tooltip does not replay the cue.

interactions.label: A BSV transaction is successfully broadcast

source_evidence.observation: tx-sent is emitted only after a successful broadcast response, not when the submit button is pressed.

steps: Submit a valid transaction in a test wallet.
browser_evidence.observation: The success receipt and tx-sent cue occur once after broadcast succeeds; rejected broadcasts use error feedback instead.

visual_alternative.description: A persistent success receipt displays the transaction id and status.

repetition.details: Re-renders and status polling deduplicate on transaction id.

interactions.label: User types in a password field

decision.reason: Per-keystroke audio is noisy, can disclose input cadence, and adds no semantic confirmation.

source_evidence.observation: The field updates form state without emitting a UI audio event.

steps: Type several characters in the password field.
browser_evidence.observation: Input remains intentionally silent while visible masked text confirms entry.

visual_alternative.description: Masked glyphs and form validation visibly reflect input state.

repetition.details: No sound is emitted for repeated text input.
